subject: Physics
chapter: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits

concepts.title: Intrinsic Semiconductors
concepts.description: Intrinsic semiconductors are pure semiconducting materials with no impurities. They have a fixed number of electrons and holes at absolute zero temperature.

concepts.title: Extrinsic Semiconductors
concepts.description: Extrinsic semiconductors are doped semiconducting materials that contain impurities, increasing their conductivity.

concepts.title: Types of Diodes
concepts.description: There are two types of diodes: p-n junction diodes and Zener diodes.

concepts.title: Mnemonics for Semiconductor Types
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'Pure Intrinsic Extrinsic' to remember the types of semiconductors: Intrinsic, Extrinsic, and Doped.

concepts.title: Doping vs Impurities
concepts.description: Students often confuse doping with impurities. Doping is the process of adding impurities to a semiconductor material.

concepts.title: Units for Resistivity
concepts.description: The unit of resistivity is ohm-meters (Ωm).

concepts.title: Zener Diode Characteristics
concepts.description: Zener diodes have a sharp reverse breakdown voltage and are used in voltage regulation circuits.

concepts.title: PN Junction Diode Symbols
concepts.description: The symbol for a p-n junction diode is a triangle with the anode at the positive end and the cathode at the negative end.
